text: is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report. Sorghum is an important grain for Africa.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音慢速英语农业报道。高粱是非洲重要的粮食作物。

text: Millions of Africans have more to eat because of Gabisa ajeta.
zh: [1] 由于加比萨·埃杰塔的努力，数百万非洲人有了更多的粮食。

text: The Ethiopian scientist developed sorghum seeds that can resist long dry periods.
zh: [2] 这位埃塞俄比亚科学家培育出了能够抵御长期干旱的高粱种子。

text: The seeds can also resist the striga weed, a big cause of crop failures in Africa.
zh: [3] 这种种子还能抵抗独脚金杂草，而独脚金是导致非洲农作物歉收的一大原因。

text: Now, his work has earned him this year's World Food Prize
zh: [4] 如今，他的研究成果为他赢得了今年的世界粮食奖。

text: from the World Food Prize Foundation in Des Moines, Iowa.
zh: [5] 该奖项由位于爱荷华州得梅因市的世界粮食奖基金会颁发。

text: Secretary of State Hillary Clinton made the announcement in Washington in June.
zh: [6] 国务卿希拉里·克林顿6月在华盛顿宣布了这一消息。

text: She pointed out that Professor Ejeta did not just develop the seeds.
zh: [7] 她指出，埃杰塔教授不仅培育出了这种种子。

text: He also worked to get them to farmers.
zh: [8] 他还努力将这些种子推广给农民使用。

text: He will receive the $250 ,000 award at a ceremony in October.
zh: [9] 他将于10月在颁奖仪式上获得25万美元的奖金。

text: He is only the second African to win the prize since it was established in 1986.
zh: [10] 自1986年该奖项设立以来，他是第二位获此殊荣的非洲人。

text: Monty Jones, a rice expert from Sierra Leone, was the winner in 2004.
zh: [11] 来自塞拉利昂的水稻专家蒙蒂·琼斯曾于2004年获奖。

text: Gabisa Ejeta is a professor at Purdue University in Indiana.
zh: [12] 加比萨·埃杰塔是美国印第安纳州普渡大学的教授。

text: Over the years, he has worked with farmers and seed companies
zh: [13] 多年来，他一直与农民和种子公司合作，

text: and developed more than 80 seed types for Africa.
zh: [14] 为非洲培育出了80多种种子。

text: In the early 1980s, Professor Ejeta
zh: [15] 20世纪80年代初，埃杰塔教授

text: developed the first sorghum hybrid seeds.
zh: [16] 培育出了第一批高粱杂交种子。

text: These resisted drought and led to a major increase in production.
zh: [17] 这些种子能够抗旱，并大幅提高了产量。

text: Drought is not the only enemy. Stryga is a parasitic weed
zh: [18] 干旱并非唯一的威胁。独脚金是一种寄生杂草，

text: that Africans commonly call witchweed. The plant attacks sorghum and other crops
zh: [19] 非洲人通常称之为“巫婆草”。这种植物侵袭高粱和其他作物，

text: and steals water and nutrients from the roots.
zh: [20] 从根部窃取水分和养分。

text: In the 1990s, Gabisa Ejeta and another Purdue researcher, Larry Butler,
zh: [21] 20世纪90年代，加比萨·埃杰塔与普渡大学另一位研究员拉里·巴特勒一起，

text: identified the complex relationships between striga and sorghum plants.
zh: [22] 揭示了独脚金与高粱之间复杂的相互关系。

text: That finding led to the development of seeds resistant to both striga and drought.
zh: [23] 这一发现促成了同时抗独脚金和抗旱种子的培育。

text: Gbisa Ejeta was raised in a one -room hut in a rural village in west -central Ethiopia.
zh: [24] 加比萨·埃杰塔在埃塞俄比亚中西部一个乡村的单间小屋中长大。

text: His mother wanted him to get an education.
zh: [25] 他的母亲希望他能接受教育。

text: He walked 20 kilometers to school in a neighboring town.
zh: [26] 他每天步行20公里，到邻近城镇上学。

text: He left home on Sunday nights and returned on Friday.
zh: [27] 他周日晚上离家，周五才回家。

text: For secondary school, he attended an agricultural and technical school.
zh: [28] 中学时期，他就读于一所农业技术学校。

text: It was established by Oklahoma State University under an American government program.
zh: [29] 这所学校是俄克拉荷马州立大学根据美国政府的一项计划创办的。

text: From there, he received an invitation to study at Purdue, where he earned a doctoral degree.
zh: [30] 从那里，他获得了普渡大学的邀请，并在那里获得了博士学位。

text: But Professor Ejeta has never forgotten his African
zh: [31] 但埃杰塔教授从未忘记自己的非洲

text: roots. Today, he encourages other scientists to turn their attention to Africa's needs.
zh: [32] 根。如今，他鼓励其他科学家将目光投向非洲的需求。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: [33] 以上就是美国之音慢速英语农业报道。

text: Transcripts and MP3s of our reports are at voaspecialenglish .com. Thank
zh: [34] 我们报道的文字稿和录音可在voaspecialenglish.com上获取。谢谢收听。
